title: Mutation-Guided Unit Test Generation with a Large Language Model
authors: Guancheng Wang, Qinghua Xu, Lionel C. Briand, Kui Liu
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Introduces MUTGEN, a mutation-guided LLM-based framework that incorporates feedback about live/uncovered mutants directly into prompts. Evaluated on 204 subjects across two benchmarks; significantly outperforms EvoSuite and vanilla LLM prompting in mutation score.

title: An Empirical Evaluation of Using Large Language Models for Automated Unit Test Generation
authors: Max Schäfer, Sarah Nadi, Aryaz Eghbali, Frank Tip
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Large-scale empirical evaluation of LLMs (TestPilot) for JavaScript unit test generation. Evaluates branch and statement coverage across 25 npm packages; outperforms traditional feedback-directed generation. Published in IEEE TSE Vol. 50 No. 1 (2024), pp. 85-105.

title: CODAMOSA: Escaping Coverage Plateaus in Test Generation with Pre-Trained Large Language Models
authors: Caroline Lemieux, Jeevana Priya Inala, Shuvendu K. Lahiri, Siddhartha Sen
venue: ICSE 2023 (IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software Engineering)
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Hybrid approach combining SBST with LLM (Codex) to escape coverage plateaus. Evaluated on 486 Python modules; achieves statistically significantly higher branch and statement coverage vs. pure SBST and LLM-only baselines.

title: Effective Test Generation Using Pre-Trained Large Language Models and Mutation Testing (MuTAP)
authors: Arghavan Moradi Dakhel, Amin Nikanjam, Vahid Majdinasab, Foutse Khomh, Michel C. Desmarais
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Introduces MuTAP, which augments LLM prompts with surviving mutant information to iteratively improve test fault-detection. Achieves mutation score of 93.57% on synthetic buggy code; detects 28% more faulty code snippets than baseline approaches.

title: ChatUniTest: A Framework for LLM-Based Test Generation
authors: Yinghao Chen, Zehao Hu, Chen Zhi, Junxiao Han, Shuiguang Deng, Jianwei Yin
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Generation-Validation-Repair framework using adaptive focal context with ChatGPT. Evaluates branch and line coverage on Java projects; improves average branch coverage from EvoSuite's 86.59% to 90.6% and line coverage from 80.02% to 89.36%.

title: HITS: High-Coverage LLM-Based Unit Test Generation via Method Slicing
authors: Zejun Wang, Kaibo Liu, Ge Li, Zhi Jin

search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Proposes method slicing to decompose complex methods into simpler slices before prompting LLMs, specifically to achieve high branch coverage in generated test suites. Evaluated on Java projects with coverage metrics.

title: CoverUp: Effective High Coverage Test Generation for Python
authors: Juan Altmayer Pizzorno, Emery D. Berger
venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Software Engineering (FSE 2025)
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Coverage-guided LLM test generation for Python using iterative feedback loops on uncovered lines and branches. Achieves 80% per-module median line+branch coverage (vs. 47% for CodaMosa); 89% overall coverage (vs. 77% for MuTAP).

title: TestGenEval: A Real World Unit Test Generation and Test Completion Benchmark
authors: Kush Jain, Gabriel Synnaeve, Baptiste RoziÃ¨re
search_string: ('large language model' OR 'LLM' OR 'GPT' OR 'ChatGPT') AND ('unit test generation' OR 'test case generation' OR 'automated unit testing') AND ('branch coverage' OR 'code coverage' OR 'mutation score' OR 'mutation testing')
abstract: Benchmark of 68647 tests across 1210 code-test file pairs from 11 Python repos. Evaluates LLMs using coverage improvement and mutation score. First benchmark to incorporate mutation score for LLM-generated test evaluation; finds GPT-4o averages only 35.2% coverage.

title: TestSpark: IntelliJ IDEA's Ultimate Test Generation Companion
authors: Arkadii Sapozhnikov, Mitchell Olsthoorn, Annibale Panichella, Vladimir Kovalenko, Pouria Derakhshanfar

abstract: IntelliJ IDEA plugin integrating EvoSuite (SBST) and LLM-based unit test generation with compilation feedback loop. Experimental comparison shows LLM-based approaches outperform traditional tools in mutation score while SBST excels in line/branch coverage.
